article_title: WHO Declares Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: WHO Declares Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern
content_preview.summary: A rare Bundibugyo virus outbreak with no licensed vaccines or treatments has been declared a PHEIC, with over 650 suspected cases and 160 suspected deaths across three DRC provinces and Uganda.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The World Health Organization declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern on 17 May 2026 over an outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. As of 22 May, [WHO](https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/ebola-virus-disease-outbreak-democratic-republic-congo-and-uganda) and the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control reported 64 confirmed cases in the DRC with 6 deaths among them, alongside over 650 suspected...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, thorough news article with clear sections covering the timeline, geographic spread, risk assessment, operational challenges, and international response. The Analysis section demonstrates appropriate editorial depth without crossing into opinion. No AI self-reference. Appropriate word count for News category (1176 words, within 400-1200 range).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources were fetched successfully (HTTP 200) and read from disk snapshots. Source-0 (source-0.html.gz, WHO PHEIC declaration): confirms PHEIC on 17 May 2026, health zones Bunia/Rwampara/Mongbwalu in Ituri Province, 2 confirmed cases in Kampala Uganda, PHEIC recommendations. Source-1 (source-1.html.gz, WHO IHR Emergency Committee first meeting 22 May): confirms meeting on 19 May, risk levels (DRC 'Very High', Uganda 'High', global 'Low'), no onward Uganda transmission as of 22 May, GeneXpert cannot detect BDBV, 'challenging operational environments' quote. Source-2 (source-2.html.gz, WHO DON602): confirms 5 May alert, 13 samples analyzed 14 May, 8 confirmed 15 May, 17th DRC outbreak declared 15 May, 2-21 day incubation period, no licensed vaccine/therapeutics quote, mortality 30-50%. Source-3 (source-3.html.gz, CDC HAN00530): confirms mortality 'approximately 25%-50%' (article states this attributed to CDC - exact match), incubation 2-21 days, risk to US low as of 18 May - all confirmed. Source-4 (source-4.html.gz, ECDC): confirms 64 confirmed DRC cases (60 Ituri, 4 North Kivu), 6 deaths, 1 South Kivu case, over 650 suspected cases/160 deaths, over 1,000 contacts followed in Ituri, Africa CDC PHECS on 18 May confirmed. ECDC EU Health Task Force activation: source states 18 May 2026, but article states '21 May' - this is a factual error requiring correction. Source-5 (source-5.html.gz, WHO DG media briefing 20 May): verbatim quote 'I determined that the situation was not a pandemic emergency, which is the new and highest classification under the amended IHR' confirmed; 'WHO assesses the risk of the epidemic as high at the national and regional levels, and low at the global level' confirmed; $3.9 million total funding confirmed; 100,000 newly displaced persons confirmed. Source-6 (source-6.html.gz, Al Jazeera): summit postponement quote confirmed, M23 claims confirmed, Dulles screening confirmed, first responders lacking supplies/US aid cuts confirmed, Uganda public transport suspension confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One factual error identified: the article states 'The ECDC activated its EU Health Task Force on 21 May to deploy experts' but the ECDC source (source-4.html.gz) clearly shows the task force activation news item is dated 18 May 2026, not 21 May. This is a subordinate body claim, not in the headline, summary, or lead. All other specific claims, figures, quotes, and dates traced correctly to cited sources. The verbatim WHO DG quote is confirmed word-for-word from source-5.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no loaded language, no editorializing. The Analysis section provides appropriate context without adopting any political position. Appropriate for a public health emergency report.
editor_notes.originality: No existing coverage of this topic found in the archive. This is the first article on the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak PHEIC. Confirmed as original.
concerns: The article states ECDC activated its EU Health Task Force on '21 May' but the ECDC source (source-4.html.gz) clearly dates this event to 18 May 2026. A corrections record must be filed.

ECDC (ecdc.europa.eu) is not currently in the source allowlist. ECDC is the European Union's official public health disease prevention and control agency, a highly credible source. The domain should be added to the allowlist as a trusted public health authority.
recommendations: Add ecdc.europa.eu to config/source_allowlist.txt as a trusted EU public health authority.
File a corrections record noting the EU Health Task Force activation date error (article says 21 May, correct date is 18 May per ECDC source).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, thoroughly sourced submission on an important and developing public health story. Seven sources from WHO, CDC, ECDC, and Al Jazeera provide comprehensive multilateral coverage. All core claims are well-sourced. One minor factual error in a subordinate body claim (ECDC task force date) warrants APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS with a public corrections record. The article should publish.
